item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I couldn't help but get distracted because this shares a lot of elements with SCP-2804 and SCP-2788.

I'm not accusing you of impropriety, but I do feel that 2788 tells basically the same high-level story but in a more interesting and charming way.

No vote, leaning towards down. It's not poorly written, it just doesn't compete within its niche.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A literally landscape-altering psychic wave of zeitgeist in the most heavily populated and one of the most historically tumultuous nations on earth? Nah, bro, that's pretty cool.
I imagine it was pretty conflicted and a lot nastier during the Cultural Revolution. A cult of personality vs. the bleak reality of being starved or shot makes for a pretty bipolar zeitgeist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wherein I try to make up for the fact my in-article explanation wasn't very clear.
3deep5u

Sorry, but there's not much of a link between this and the other things that I have written (although that may change soon); the connections between this and 1474/2214 mostly have to do with the fact that I have a fever that can only be cured by writing about socialism and mysterious, incomprehensible forces. Also thanks!

The SCP-2387 phenomenon is essentially the physical manifestation from the psychic flotsam of rapid changes in Chinese society.

Billions, if not trillions, of conversations, debates, revolutionary slogans, advertising slogans, and half-remembered thoughts, all regarding how the world is changing and how it should be changed collide in an infinity of different ways over seconds, hours, days, and years. Eventually, the sheer weight of all of this stuff coalesces into an entity. In the sixties, it was revolutionary fervor. In the eighties onward, it became neoliberalism. Hence the bastardized (and apocryphal) Deng quote about how "to get rich is glorious" and the talk about how "this was all stalls" (presumably the "this" in whatever conversation was being cannibalized is now luxury apartments)

The entity responsible for SCP-2387 is not something capable of any sort of thought or even choice as we would recognize it; it operates on blind instinct more than anything. And that instinct says that the new paradigm must be manifested in terms of buildings. In the sixties, this meant replacing peasant huts with the communal farms made from the Unreinforced Concrete of the People, while today, it means whatever the most obscenely expensive kinds of buildings on the market today.

So yeah, SCP-2387 is not some extra-dimensional entity, or some god trying and failing to understand humanity. It is people and the way that they think.

Unless you have a cooler idea, in which case lol jk, your idea is actually the one that I meant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Damn, interest piqued. The raving from the anomaly seems to indicate it's some kind of representation of progress, or counter-revolutionary spirit… is there some clue I'm missing in the article, or just I go combing through your body of work to satiate my vaguely obsessive-compulsive curiosity?
Edit: went through your author page. The causal link between the real-estate market and the anomaly is kind of reminiscent of -2214, and the Chinese economic/political angle might imply a link with -1474…anything there? Well, moving on to your tales, anyway. This is an awesome body of work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Without wanting to give too much away, it is a force that is very much of this world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay that makes sense now to me, but this is pretty freaky +1 for me because of that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2387 is a phenomenon affecting the rapid construction and demolition of buildings located within three contiguous square blocks in the Shijingshan District of Beijing.

They just monitor those three square blocks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have a question before I give it my +1.

How does (or did) the Foundation identify the buildings being affected before the Graffiti appears? Are they scouring the archives to find instances of the aforementioned company?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, an otherworldly force of raving lunacy that also happens to be a demolition-happy realtor?
…Is this very subtly about Donald Trump?
Just kidding. Cool skip. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you are a funny, scary human

also I liked the article nice job man +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why did I find that very funny?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, I would like to thank Vezaz, Sunny, and Aiden Eldritch for reading this and suggesting changes. Based in part on reading the book The Last Days of Old Beijing by Michael Meyer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fix your -XXXXs, and when you do that I'll give you a +1
